search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae osteoarthritis telehealth guideline PDF hip
site:doh.gov.ae hip pain guideline Abu Dhabi
rejection_reasons: The workflow is broad, so only exact OA-compatible content was mapped.
Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medication content was outside this mapping.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for middle-aged and older adults with OA-compatible hip symptoms, not acute trauma or every hip-pain cause.
setting_applicability: Partial: Dubai telehealth guideline versus setting-agnostic baseline with in-person special tests.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates; broader UAE application is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official Issue 2 copy dated 2024-02-21, effective 2024-04-21, revision due 2029-02-21, opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: Issue 2 is current on the DHA telehealth guidance portal.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped usage-related pain, stiffness, range, tenderness, function, fever, night pain, and systemic concern fields in OA-compatible presentations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports usage-related joint pain, stiffness, motion restriction, tenderness, swelling, instability, functional limitation, night pain, fever, redness or heat, weight loss, and systemic concern features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped hip, groin, thigh, walking, stiffness, range-of-motion, and internal-rotation fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes knee pain location, stairs-related pain, giving way, instability, and hip groin or thigh pain, stiffness, walking pain, restricted movement, and early loss of internal rotation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports OA terminology and selected X-ray or MRI review without inferring diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines clinical diagnosis in typical adults, cautions that radiographic findings may not match symptoms, and limits MRI or other imaging to selected uncertainty or alternative-diagnosis contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped worsening, night-symptom, imaging, and clinician-arranged review documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines concern and specialist contexts including a hot swollen joint, persistent fever, night symptoms, weight loss, face-to-face assessment needs, worsening symptoms, severe functional limitation, and selected imaging.

unresolved_source_gaps: Acute trauma, inability to bear weight, neurovascular symptoms, groin swelling, Thomas, Trendelenburg, FABER/FADIR, walking-aid advice, medication, and fixed follow-up remain unsupported.
The osteoarthritis label must remain clinician-stated and age/context dependent.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
